com: Formal offer by means of scheme of arrangement announced at 590p per share plus 22 pence dividend. 99.9% of votes were in favour at meetings on 16 April 2026.

Recommended final cash offer by private equity firm EQT at £60 per share. The offer will be implemented by means of a Court-sanctioned scheme of arrangement and is expected to become effective in Q4 2026 or Q1 2027.
Scheme of arrangement made at same terms as indicative proposal. Beazley shareholder vote passed on 22 April 2026. Expected to become effective during H2 2026.
Shareholders have overwhelmingly approved the scheme, but still subject to regulatory approval. Completion expected some time in Q3.

Formal offer (scheme) announced on 7 April 2026 by Tinicum and Blackstone at 297.85p in cash. Overwhelmingly approved by shareholders on 26 May 2026. Indicative offer of 270p from Advent was rejected. Advent withdrew on 8 April 2026. Arcline Investment Management had made an approach before withdrawing on 1 April 2026.

Indicative offer by Toscafund (which already owns 24.36%) at 250p per share, with unlisted share alternative, which Board is "minded to recommend". Approaches had been previously received from Bridgepoint and Triton, following a strategic review but talks ended.

In preliminary discussions with a number of interested counterparties. Potential offerors not required to be identified, but Providence Equity Partners was named on 13 May 2026 (PUSU deadline 10 June). Epiris and separate Oakley Capital/Giacom consortium named on 15 May 2026 but Oakley Capital/Giacom withdrew on 12 June 2026. On 10 June 2026, Providence & Epiris formed a consortium with a PUSU deadline of 8 July 2026. On 24 June 2026, Providence announced that it was no longer part of the Consortium and Epiris was considering its options.
Recommended offer (scheme) on previous terms as indicative offer. Undertakings in respect of 45.58%. IIG shareholders would own approximately 99.01% of combined group. Completion expected mid August.
Shareholders approved scheme on 11 March and regulatory approval received. Will become effective on 4 August.
Drax makes formal offer on 1 June 2026, by means of scheme. Offer follows strategic review and formal sales process.

b: LONDONMETRIC PROPERTY & SCHRODER REAL ESTATE INVESTMENT TRUST
com: LondonMetric Property PLC and Schroder REIT make indicative all share offer, after strategic review. 46% in LondonMetric shares and 54% in Schroder REIT shares. As proposal is part of formal sale process, no PUSU deadline.

Helios consortium (which already owns 45.11%) has gone hostile, with 85p formal offer. StoneX had made an indicative offer of 110p, before withdrawing on 5 May, given the Helios Consortium's lack of support.

Formal £235.2m offer by Charterhouse Capital, 41.7% undertakings. Scheme of arrangement in cash with a rollover alternative. Approved by shareholders on 8 June 2026. Effective date expected to be 30 July 2026.
Multiple unsolicited proposals from Saudi Arabia's Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah, which has due diligence access and a PUSU deadline of 29 July. Genel Energy plc made a recommended offer (scheme) on 2 July.
Strategic review - in discussions with two parties.
Strategic review & formal sale process. Has not received an indicative offer but has held preliminary discussions with a small number of counterparties.
At “minded to recommend” stage. Due diligence at an advanced stage. We expect a formal offer.
Formal sale process. Discussions with potential bidders ongoing.
Discussions are with selected third parties as part of a private strategic review process.

Formal offer by NEO NEXT+ (scheme). Follows discussions with three parties, being (i) Capricorn Energy PLC ("Capricorn Energy"), (ii) Petrogas International E&P Coöperatief U.A., a subsidiary of Mohammed Al Barwani LLC, and (iii) Blue Concept Hld AS, all of which had withdrawn by June 2026. Previous scheme by Viaro Energy had lapsed on 31 March 2026.
Formal offer by means of scheme of arrangement. Shareholders have approved scheme and all regulatory clearances granted.

Formal cash offer (scheme) from US ingredients group Ingredion Inc, valuing each share at up to 615p - 595p in cash plus permitted final and interim dividends of up to 20p. Follows proposal at same price.
Mixed cash and share offer, with support of 50.14% of shareholders. Expected to complete during the third quarter of 2026.

On 12 June 2026, Glenstone REIT (which already owns 24%) announced an unsolicited offer at a price of 70p per share, rejected by target Board. On 6 July 2026, Glenstone announced a final cash offer of 71.4p per share with an acceptance condition of 50%, once again rejected by the Board.
AEWU considering an offer to acquire AIRE for 0.725 AEWU shares for each AIRE share. The offer follows discussions with AIRE Board earlier this year when an agreement could not be reached.
Recommended cash offer (scheme), with undertakings in relation to 0.34% . Follows previous approach by TA Associates, which withdrew on 18 May 2026

Castlelake had made three unsolicited proposals to the Easyjet Board since 12 June 2026, all of which had been rejected by the Easyjet Board. On 6 July 2026, the parties announced agreement in principle on a recommended cash offer of 690p per share, including a partial unlisted share alternative.
Apollo has emerged with a higher 715p/share cash possible offer vs Castlelake’s latest 690p, and easyJet’s board is now minded to recommend.
Recommended all share takeover offer

Unsolicited indicative offer from Railpen which already holds 18.4% of the shares in IP Group. Comprises 59p in cash, a mechanism to enable IP Group shareholders to realise the value of IP's shareholding in Oxford Nanopore Technologies plc (valued at approximately 10.7 pence per IP Group share), and a contingent value right of up to 5p per share. The possible offer has been rejected by the Board.

On 23 June 2026, FirstCash announced a recommended cash offer of 600p per share plus dividends of up to 9p per share, to be effected by scheme of arrangement. On 16 July 2026, FirstCash announced a final recommended cash offer (scheme) for Ramsdens of 675p per share plus dividend of up to 9p per share.

Mandatory final cash offer by Hena Holdings Ltd for the remaining 50.38% of the shares at US$0.50 per share. IDH's Independent Directors state that the price of US$0.50 per share does not reflect the underlying inherent value of the company and recommend the shareholders not to accept the offer
Recommended cash offer of 23.07p per share plus special dividends of 4p and ordinary dividend of 0.9317p per share, to be effected by scheme of arrangement

Unsolicited indicative proposal of 0.084 new Prologis shares for every SEGRO share with an implied price of 925p per share based on the Prologis share price of US$145.3 and a GBP:USD exchange rate of 1.32. The offer has been rejected by the SEGRO Board.

Unsolicited indicative proposal by Brave Bison Group plc for the acquisition of the remaining 72% of System1 for 68p per share in cash and 2.7553 new Brave Bison shares. The System1 Board believes that it materially undervalues System1
Formal sale process. The Board is not in any active discussions with any potential offeror

Recommended cash offer (scheme) of £1,230p per share plus interim dividend of 4.9p per share for all shares G&H by  Greenlight Bidco Limited, an acquisition vehicle owned by Arlington Capital Partners VII, L.P.
Recommended cash offer (scheme) of 506p per share plus dividend of up to 3p per share for all shares in Rotork by ABB Automation Holding UK Limited, a subsidiary of ABB Ltd.
